title_zh: Claude遇见Blackwell Ultra：Anthropic模型现已在Azure的NVIDIA GB300上运行
title_en: Claude Meets Blackwell Ultra: Anthropic’s Models Now Run on NVIDIA GB300 in Azure
summary_zh: Anthropic的Claude模型现已在微软Foundry平台上一般可用，该平台托管于微软Azure，运行在NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPU上，为Azure原生企业提供了构建自主和领域特定AI代理的新强大途径。随着代理式AI推动企业创新并变得更加自主，组织需要更强的计算能力。
summary_en: Anthropic’s Claude models are now generally available on Microsoft Foundry hosted on Microsoft Azure, running on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs. This provides Azure-native enterprises a powerful new way to build autonomous and domain-specific AI agents. As agentic AI drives enterprise innovation and becomes more autonomous, organizations require enhanced computing access.
why_it_matters_zh: 通过将Claude模型在性能领先的NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra GPU上部署，Anthropic和微软Azure能够为企业客户提供更高效、定制化的AI解决方案，助力企业智能化转型和创新。
why_it_matters_en: Deploying Claude models on high-performance NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra GPUs via Microsoft Azure enables Anthropic to offer enterprises more efficient and tailored AI solutions, facilitating intelligent transformation and innovation in business.

title_zh: 通过持出选择实现递归自我进化代理
title_en: Recursive Self-Evolving Agents via Held-Out Selection
summary_zh: 本文介绍了递归自我进化代理（RSEA），它通过携带一个紧凑的三层自然语言状态（命令策略、可重用技能和程序化剧本）来改写自身策略。RSEA使用持出集进行严格的保留更好策略筛选，确保在四个多样的基准测试上都能稳定提升性能。实验表明，没有单一方法能在所有任务中通吃；不受控的上下文进化风险较大，而RSEA的持出选择机制保证了递归自我进化的安全性和稳健性。
summary_en: The paper introduces Recursive Self-Evolving Agent (RSEA), which maintains a compact three-layer natural language state comprising an imperative strategy, reusable skills, and a procedural playbook. RSEA rewrites each layer from its own experience across generations and commits a candidate only if it does not regress on a disjoint held-out split, ensuring monotone performance improvements. Evaluated on four diverse benchmarks and against six baselines, no artifact universally wins; unguarded context evolution is unstable, while RSEA's strict held-out selection enables safe recursive self-evolution without significant performance degradation.
why_it_matters_zh: RSEA通过结合严格的持出验证机制，创新性地解决了递归自我进化中性能回退的问题，提升了多种任务上的稳健表现，具有重要的理论意义和应用价值。
why_it_matters_en: RSEA innovatively addresses the challenge of performance regression in recursive self-evolution by employing strict held-out validation, resulting in more robust improvements across diverse tasks, advancing both theory and practice of evolving language-conditioned agents.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Recursive Self-Evolving Agents via Held-Out Selection. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 71.

title_zh: 模型能力提升的数据与评估闭环
title_en: Data and Evaluation Closed-Loop for Model Capability Enhancement
summary_zh: 该论文提出了一种名为“能力切片”的方法，通过将评估样本按背景条件、任务类型、解决操作和输出限制进行分组，构建评估与数据之间的闭环。这一闭环使得基准测试中发现的模型失败能够被精准定位并转换为针对性的数据干预，从而提升模型能力。论文通过两个案例验证了该方法的有效性：一个案例通过恢复特定损失改善了BBH性能，另一个案例通过针对性采样显著提升了数学推理任务的表现，显示该闭环推断过程可常规化、可审计且可验证。
summary_en: This paper introduces the concept of "capability slices," grouping evaluation samples by background condition, task type, solving operation, and output constraint to form a closed loop between evaluation and data. This loop enables precise localization of benchmark failures and their transformation into targeted data interventions to enhance model capability. Validated through two case studies, one restoring a specific masked loss to improve BBH performance and another applying targeted sampling to boost math reasoning tasks, demonstrating the evaluation-to-data inference can be routine, auditable, and experimentally validated rather than intuitive.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究解决了评估反馈与训练数据之间词汇不兼容、推断依赖直觉的问题，通过能力切片及评估与数据分类体系形成闭环，使模型能力提升的过程更加系统化和可验证，具有重要实践价值。
why_it_matters_en: This work addresses the mismatch between evaluation feedback and training data vocabularies, moving beyond intuition-based inference by creating a closed loop through capability slices and taxonomies, thereby systematizing and validating the model capability enhancement process with significant practical implications.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Data and Evaluation Closed-Loop for Model Capability Enhancement. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 77.

title_zh: GPTNT：在《Keep Talking and Nobody Explodes》游戏中多模态智能体实时协作基准测试
title_en: GPTNT: Benchmarking Real-Time Collaboration Between Multimodal Agents on Keep Talking And Nobody Explodes
summary_zh: GPTNT是一个基于合作视频游戏《Keep Talking and Nobody Explodes》的新基准测试，旨在评估多模态模型在实时异步且受限沟通条件下与人类或其他智能体协作拆除炸弹的能力。测试中，一个智能体能操作炸弹但无拆弹说明，另一个持有说明却看不到炸弹，二者需有效沟通才能成功拆弹。当前最先进模型对该任务表现不佳，难以实时拆除炸弹，暴露了状态追踪、时间压力下的高效行动、歧义处理和错误恢复等关键弱点。GPTNT支持程序化生成和社区扩展，提供持续演进的协作性能评估平台。
summary_en: GPTNT is a new benchmark based on the cooperative video game Keep Talking and Nobody Explodes, designed to evaluate multimodal models' ability to collaborate in real-time asynchronous conditions with limited communication to defuse bombs. One agent can manipulate the bomb but lacks defusal instructions, while the other holds the instructions but cannot see the bomb; effective communication is essential for success. State-of-the-art models fail to defuse any bombs in real-time, revealing critical weaknesses in state tracking, efficient actions under time pressure, ambiguity handling, and error recovery. GPTNT supports procedural generation and inherits a modding community, enabling an evolving benchmark for collaborative performance.
why_it_matters_zh: GPTNT揭示了多模态模型在现实复杂协作环境中的不足，强调了模拟真实时间压力和信息不对称的重要性，并通过基于真实游戏的环境实现程序化多样性，推动协作智能体评测的发展。
why_it_matters_en: GPTNT exposes the limitations of multimodal models in realistic, complex collaborative scenarios, highlighting the importance of simulating real-time pressure and information asymmetry. By leveraging a real game environment with procedural diversity, it advances the evaluation of collaborative intelligence.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: GPTNT: Benchmarking Real-Time Collaboration Between Multimodal Agents on Keep Talking And Nobody Explodes. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 76.

title_zh: 极限生成与无穷幻觉现象
title_en: Generating in the Limit with Infinitely Many Hallucinations
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种新的语言生成极限模型，允许学习器在生成过程中出现无限多错误，但其错误频率趋近于零，从而保持高精度。研究分析了枚举、新颖性和有效性等限制条件下的生成问题，展示了在对手隐瞒大量目标语言时，该松弛模型可以显著提高召回率。此工作推动了语言生成模型向更贴近实际场景的方向发展，容忍偶尔错误和重复但控制其频率。
summary_en: This work introduces a novel framework for language generation in the limit that permits infinitely many mistakes by the learner as long as their frequency tends to zero, maintaining precision at one. It analyzes generation under constraints on enumeration, novelty, and validity, showing that this relaxation can increase recall when much of the target language is withheld by an adversary. Overall, the study advances understanding toward more realistic language generation scenarios where occasional errors and repetitions are inevitable but controlled.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究挑战了传统语言识别模型的严格有效性要求，提出允许无限错误但频率受控的新理论，契合现代大语言模型生成中的实际情况，有助于提升生成模型的召回能力和实用性。
why_it_matters_en: This research challenges strict validity criteria in classic language identification by allowing infinitely many controlled errors, aligning theory with practical scenarios in modern large language models, thus potentially improving recall and applicability of generation models.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Generating in the Limit with Infinitely Many Hallucinations. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 58.

title_zh: 从阿拉伯语-英语机读词典中提取知识的信息抽取方法
title_en: Extracting Knowledge from an Arabic-English Machine-Readable Dictionary Using Information Extraction
summary_zh: 本研究提出了一种自动方法，从阿拉伯语-英语机读版Al-Mawrid词典中提取词汇信息。通过n元语法分析和关键词上下文分析发现形态、句法和语义模式，再利用手工规则进行信息抽取。同时结合标点符号和启发式方法提取同义词。结果显示，高精度提取所有信息类型，高召回提取同义词，其他类型召回较低。Al-Mawrid词典中含有丰富的派生词（形态信息）、同义词、领域标签及下位/上位关系（语义信息）。
summary_en: This study presents an automatic method to extract lexical information from the Arabic-English machine-readable Al-Mawrid dictionary. Using n-gram and keyword-in-context analyses, morphological, syntactic, and semantic patterns were discovered and extracted through handcrafted rule-based information extraction. Punctuation and heuristics were used to extract synonyms. The method achieved high precision across all information types, high recall for synonyms, and lower recall for other information. The Al-Mawrid dictionary contains significant morphological derivations, synonyms, domain labels, and hyponym/hypernym semantic relations.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究突破了语言知识获取瓶颈，利用自动化方法从资源型词典中高效提取多层次语言信息，推动了自然语言处理在阿拉伯语-英语双语资源的应用与发展。
why_it_matters_en: The research addresses the knowledge acquisition bottleneck by automatically extracting multilayered linguistic information from resource-rich dictionaries, advancing NLP applications in Arabic-English bilingual resources.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Extracting Knowledge from an Arabic-English Machine-Readable Dictionary Using Information Extraction. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 60.

title_zh: 现代BERT模型的法律领域适应
title_en: Legal Domain Adaptation of Modern BERT Models
summary_zh: 本文研究了现代BERT模型在法律领域的领域适应。通过在所有美国法院判例上进一步使用掩码语言模型目标对ModernBERT进行预训练，发现尽管该模型已比原始BERT训练多约500倍数据，但在法律领域的进一步预训练依然带来显著提升，尤其是在涉及美国法院判例的数据集上。此外，从零开始的预训练效果不及基于现有ModernBERT检查点的进一步预训练。所得到的模型支持处理最长8,192个标记的序列，能够生成有意义的法律段落嵌入，也可快速对数百条法律段落进行重排序。所有模型检查点均已公开发布。
summary_en: This paper investigates domain adaptation of modern BERT models for the legal domain. By further pre-training ModernBERT on all US court opinions using masked language modeling, significant improvements are achieved over vanilla ModernBERT on US court opinion-related datasets. Despite having been trained on roughly 500 times more data than original BERT, the model still benefits from domain-specific further pre-training. Training from scratch does not outperform further pre-training an existing checkpoint. The resulting models can handle sequences up to 8,192 tokens, enabling meaningful embeddings and fast re-ranking of legal passages. All model checkpoints are publicly released.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究展示了在法律领域对现代BERT模型进行领域适应和进一步预训练的重要性与效果，尤其在处理长文本和具体法律数据时表现优越，推动法律文本处理技术的发展。
why_it_matters_en: This research highlights the importance and effectiveness of domain adaptation and further pre-training of modern BERT models in the legal domain, particularly for long sequences and specific legal data, advancing technology for legal text processing.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Legal Domain Adaptation of Modern BERT Models. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: IMCBench：用于图像辅助医学对话的多模态大语言模型基准
title_en: IMCBench: A benchmark for multimodal LLMs in Image-grounded Medical Conversations
summary_zh: 近期大语言模型和视觉语言模型的进展促进了多模态数据推理，可应用于临床决策支持与分诊。目前医学AI基准测试存在碎片化问题：部分支持多轮对话但缺少图像，部分有多模态输入但仅关注单轮问答。为此，IMCBench提出结合真实临床图像与合成患者资料的多轮对话基准，模拟真实患者与临床医生交流，评估安全性、准确性及诊断不确定性使用。基于专家注释校准的LLM评审，评测四大模型家族的8个多模态模型，Claude Opus 4.6表现最佳（3.61分），但无模型在所有维度均领先，且恶性及罕见病症安全性均有所下降。消融实验显示视觉输入与电子健康记录上下文均显著提升指导安全性。研究表明，准确的临床描述并不确保病患安全，强调医学AI多维度评估框架的必要性。
summary_en: Recent advances in large language and vision-language models have enabled reasoning over multimodal data for clinical decision support and triaging. Existing medical AI benchmarks are fragmented, either lacking images in multi-turn dialogues or focusing only on single-turn QA with multimodal inputs. IMCBench introduces an image-grounded, multi-turn medical conversation benchmark pairing real clinical images with synthetic patient profiles to simulate realistic interactions. Evaluations cover safety, accuracy, and appropriate uncertainty usage. Eight state-of-the-art multimodal models were benchmarked, with Claude Opus 4.6 scoring highest overall but no model excelling in all dimensions, and safety degrading for malignant and rare conditions. Ablation studies highlight the critical role of both visual input and EHR context in safe guidance. Findings demonstrate that accurate clinical descriptions alone do not guarantee patient safety, underscoring the need for multi-dimensional evaluation in medical AI.
why_it_matters_zh: IMCBench填补了医学AI评测中多模态多轮对话缺乏图像和现实语境的空白，推动更全面、更安全的临床AI应用验证。评测揭示现有模型在安全维度存在不足，提示未来需整合视觉与电子健康信息，提高模型安全性和可靠性。该工作推动了医学AI评价标准的多维度发展，对临床决策辅助工具的开发有重要指导意义。
why_it_matters_en: IMCBench addresses gaps in medical AI evaluation by introducing an image-grounded, multi-turn conversation benchmark that simulates realistic clinical scenarios. The study reveals current multimodal models' limitations in safety, especially for serious conditions, emphasizing the importance of visual and health record context for safe guidance. This benchmark promotes more holistic and rigorous evaluation frameworks in medical AI, guiding future development of safer and more reliable clinical decision support systems.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: IMCBench: A benchmark for multimodal LLMs in Image-grounded Medical Conversations. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 76.
